content.question: What is the true causal effect of the 6- and 8-year track *structure itself* (independent of admissions selectivity, school reputation, or residential sorting) on between-school segregation and inequality of opportunity?
content.reduction_path: Conduct a quasi-experimental or instrumental-variable study isolating the marginal effect of track-age entry points (e.g., using geographic variation in track availability or policy discontinuities at administrative boundaries) from school-choice and family-background confounders; or implement a randomized pilot in which some cohorts are assigned to unified-intake schools while matched cohorts experience current tracking, with long-term outcome tracking through tertiaryeducation and labor-market entries.
